statement.markdown: The provided bubble sort implementation contains a logical bug. Identify and fix the bug to ensure the function correctly sorts an array of integers in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a list of integers, e.g. [5, 2, 8, 1, 9]
statement.notes: Do not use any built-in sort functions.

constraints.description: List of integers to be sorted

hints.text: The logic of the provided bubble sort implementation seems correct at first glance, but there might be a small mistake that's causing it to fail for certain inputs.

editorial.title: Correcting the Bubble Sort Algorithm
editorial.intuition: The provided bubble sort implementation uses two nested loops to compare and swap adjacent elements in the array. However, it might not be handling the case where the input array has duplicate elements correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would involve implementing the bubble sort algorithm from scratch, which would involve comparing and swapping adjacent elements in the array until no more swaps are needed.
editorial.optimalSolution: An optimal solution would be to modify the existing bubble sort implementation to correctly handle the case where the input array has duplicate elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected bubble sort implementation should be able to correctly sort an array of integers in ascending order.
